input.topic: Proposed closure of three fire stations in the northern district
input.angle: Whether the closures will increase response times in areas with the highest fire risk
input.notes: Council says closures save 2.4 million per year. Fire union says response times will increase by 3-5 minutes in affected areas. 2023 fire at a care home in the district killed two residents - response time was already 11 minutes. Council data shows call volume down 15% over five years. Union disputes methodology. Public consultation closes next month. A retired chief fire officer has written to the local paper opposing the plan.

input.topic: Opening of new community arts centre in former industrial building
input.angle: How the building was funded and what it offers
input.notes: Cost 4.5 million. Lottery funding plus council contribution. Opens next month. Will include gallery, workshop spaces, and a cafe. Building was a former textiles factory.

input.topic: Belgium's plan to phase out nuclear power by 2025, then reversed
input.angle: Why the government reversed its position and what it means for energy policy
input.notes: Original plan passed in 2003. Extended twice. In 2022 government agreed to keep two reactors open until 2035 due to energy security concerns after Russia invaded Ukraine. Engie/Electrabel operates the plants. The Green party was in the coalition but accepted the extension. Cost of lifetime extension estimated at 2 billion euros. Belgium imports electricity from France during peak demand. Anti-nuclear groups say the reversal undermines renewables investment. Pro-nuclear groups say Belgium cannot meet climate targets without nuclear. EU taxonomy now classifies nuclear as sustainable under certain conditions.
